body: Slicex analyzes a loop for transients and cuts it into individual slices you can trigger from the keyboard or the piano roll. A one-click dump writes the original groove out as MIDI, so you can re-sequence, swap or quantize the slices without re-chopping by hand.

Eight articulation slots hold filter and envelope settings that can be assigned to individual slices, letting one loop carry several distinct playback voices - for example a filtered stab on one slice and a clean hit on another.
